blocks.text: First pass through the clippings is done. The short version: the coverage leans far more on the leadership turnover than on anything operational, which is not the balance the investor FAQ assumes, so I would not take the FAQ framing at face value going in. That is the one assumption worth flagging before we build the outline on top of it.

On the transcripts, yes, pull them. Two of the clippings quote the earnings calls directly and I want the quotes checked against the record before either turns into a finding. If Robert Smith is already sending the board-deck excerpts, the transcripts can ride along in the same request.

The Gallegos, Pope and Marsh set is thin, as Michelle Rose said, but thin in a useful way: it means the {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}} narrative is not fixed yet, which is exactly what an audit at this stage hopes to find.
